---
title: "data_cleaning"
output: html_document
---


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
library(tidyverse)
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stderr}

```
── Attaching core tidyverse packages ──────────────────────── tidyverse 2.0.0 ──
✔ dplyr     1.2.1     ✔ readr     2.2.0
✔ forcats   1.0.1     ✔ stringr   1.6.0
✔ ggplot2   4.0.3     ✔ tibble    3.3.1
✔ lubridate 1.9.5     ✔ tidyr     1.3.2
✔ purrr     1.2.2     
── Conflicts ────────────────────────────────────────── tidyverse_conflicts() ──
✖ dplyr::filter() masks stats::filter()
✖ dplyr::lag()    masks stats::lag()
ℹ Use the conflicted package (<http://conflicted.r-lib.org/>) to force all conflicts to become errors
```


:::

```{.r .cell-code}
library(dplyr)
```
:::



::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
df <- read_csv("/cloud/project/data/tPBM_data.csv")
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stderr}

```
Rows: 102 Columns: 18
── Column specification ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Delimiter: ","
chr (13): study_id, author, condition, population_type, population_age, outc...
dbl  (5): year, wavelength_nm, session_duration_min, session_count, sample

ℹ Use `spec()` to retrieve the full column specification for this data.
ℹ Specify the column types or set `show_col_types = FALSE` to quiet this message.
```


:::

```{.r .cell-code}
df_clean <- df |>
  #drop exact duplicate rows
  distinct() |>
# drop near- duplicates: same authors + year + condition + sample size 
  distinct(author, year, condition, sample, .keep_all= TRUE) |>
    mutate(
      year = as.integer(year),
      wavelength_nm = as.numeric(wavelength_nm),
      session_duration_min = as.numeric(session_duration_min),
      session_count= as.numeric(session_count),
      sample = as.integer(sample),
      application = str_replace(application, "transcrainial", "transcranial"),
      population_type= str_to_lower(str_trim(population_type)),
      blinding= if_else(is.na(blinding) | blinding =="", "not reported", blinding), 
      outcome_direction = str_to_lower(str_trim(outcome_direction)),
      study_design= str_to_title(str_trim(study_design)),
      source = str_trim(str_remove(source, " - PubMed$| -PMC$| - ScienceDirect$")),
      condition= str_replace_all(condition, "[,/]", ";"), #normalize all delims to ; b4 splitting 
      outcome_cat= str_replace_all(outcome_cat, "[,/]", ";"),
      #fix typo 
      condition = str_replace_all(condition, regex("opoid", ignore_case = TRUE), "Opioid")
    ) |>
    
    #split conditions into separate rows 
    separate_longer_delim(condition, delim = ";") |>
  mutate(condition = str_trim(condition)) |>
  filter(condition != "", !is.na(condition)) |> #added empty string filters to catch blank rows created by splitting
  mutate(condition = case_when(
    str_detect(condition, regex("alzheimer|dementia.*alzheimer|alzheimer.*dementia", ignore_case = TRUE)) ~ "Alzheimer's",
    str_detect(condition, regex("dementia", ignore_case = TRUE)) ~ "Dementia",
    str_detect(condition, regex("mild cognitive impairment|^mci$", ignore_case = TRUE)) ~ "Mild Cognitive Impairment",
    str_detect(condition, regex("major depress|^mdd$|^depression$", ignore_case = TRUE)) ~ "Major Depressive Disorder",
    str_detect(condition, regex("post.stroke depression", ignore_case = TRUE)) ~ "Post-Stroke Depression",
    str_detect(condition, regex("traumatic brain injury|brain injury|^tbi$", ignore_case = TRUE)) ~ "Brain Injury",
    str_detect(condition, regex("parkinson", ignore_case = TRUE)) ~ "Parkinson's",
    str_detect(condition, regex("autism spectrum|^asd$", ignore_case = TRUE)) ~ "Autism Spectrum Disorder",
    str_detect(condition, regex("^adhd$|attention deficit", ignore_case = TRUE)) ~ "ADHD",
    str_detect(condition, regex("seasonal affective", ignore_case = TRUE)) ~ "Seasonal Affective Disorder",
    str_detect(condition, regex("opioid use disorder|opoid use disorder|opiod craving", ignore_case = TRUE)) ~ "Opioid Use Disorder",
    str_detect(condition, regex("bipolar", ignore_case = TRUE)) ~ "Bipolar Disorder",
    str_detect(condition, regex("anxiety", ignore_case = TRUE)) ~ "Anxiety Disorder",
    str_detect(condition, regex("insomnia|poor sleep", ignore_case = TRUE)) ~ "Insomnia",
    str_detect(condition, regex("fibromyalgia", ignore_case = TRUE)) ~ "Fibromyalgia",
    str_detect(condition, regex("schizophrenia", ignore_case = TRUE)) ~ "Schizophrenia",
    str_detect(condition, regex("cognitive dysfunction|brain fog|post.covid", ignore_case = TRUE)) ~ "Cognitive Dysfunction",
    str_detect(condition, regex("sexual dysfunction", ignore_case = TRUE)) ~ "Sexual Dysfunction",
    str_detect(condition, regex("alcohol use", ignore_case = TRUE)) ~ "Alcohol Use Disorder",
    str_detect(condition, regex("natural aging|aging|cognitive decline", ignore_case = TRUE)) ~ "Natural Aging",
    str_detect(condition, regex("healthy|non.clinical", ignore_case = TRUE)) ~ "Healthy",
    str_detect(condition, regex("Obsessive-Compulsive Disorder", ignore_case = TRUE)) ~ "OCD",
    str_detect(condition, regex("^ptsd$|post.traumatic", ignore_case = TRUE)) ~ "PTSD",
    str_detect(condition, regex("chronic pain|neuropathic pain", ignore_case = TRUE)) ~ "Chronic Pain",
    #dropping mood as condition
    str_detect(condition, regex("^mood$", ignore_case = TRUE)) ~ NA_character_,
    TRUE ~ condition
  )) |>
  
  distinct(study_id, condition, .keep_all = TRUE) |> #dedup after condition split
    
    #split outcome_cat into separate rows
    separate_longer_delim(outcome_cat, delim = ";") |>
    mutate(outcome_cat = str_trim(outcome_cat)) |>
  filter(outcome_cat != "", !is.na(outcome_cat)) |> #added empty string filters to catch blank rows
    mutate(outcome_cat = tools::toTitleCase(tolower(outcome_cat))) |>
  mutate(outcome_cat = case_when(
    str_detect(outcome_cat, regex("ptsd Symptoms", ignore_case = TRUE)) ~ "PTSD Symptoms",
    str_detect(outcome_cat, regex("eeg|brain oscillations", ignore_case = TRUE)) ~ "Brain Oscillations",
    str_detect(outcome_cat, regex("ocd symptoms", ignore_case = TRUE)) ~ "OCD Symptoms",
    str_detect(outcome_cat, regex("adhd symptoms", ignore_case = TRUE)) ~ "ADHD Symptoms",
    str_detect(outcome_cat, regex("memory|visual working memory|working memory", ignore_case = TRUE)) ~ "Memory",
    str_detect(outcome_cat, regex("sleep quality|daytime sleepiness|wakefulness", ignore_case = TRUE)) ~ "Sleep",
    str_detect(outcome_cat, regex("opioid craving|addiction|drug cravings", ignore_case = TRUE)) ~ "Addiction/Cravings",
    str_detect(outcome_cat, regex("brain hemodynamics|cerebral oxygenation", ignore_case = TRUE)) ~ "Brain Hemodynamics",
    str_detect(outcome_cat, regex("social behaivor", ignore_case = TRUE)) ~ "Social Behavior",
    str_detect(outcome_cat, regex("cognitive function|executive function", ignore_case = TRUE)) ~ "Cognitive Function",
    TRUE ~ outcome_cat))|>
  
  filter(!is.na(condition)) |> #this will drop the NA columns where mood was 
  
  distinct(study_id, condition, outcome_cat, .keep_all = TRUE) |> # dedupe after outcome_cat split
  
    #selecting the final dataset
    select(
      study_id, year, author, condition, population_type, population_age,
      study_design, application,
      wavelength_nm, session_duration_min, session_count,
      blinding, control_type,
      outcome_cat, outcome_direction,
      sample, source, publisher
  ) |>
  arrange(year, author, condition, outcome_cat)

write_csv(df_clean, "tpbm_clean.csv")
```
:::


Viewing clean dataset


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
view(df_clean)
```
:::


Viewing unique values: 

::: {.cell}


```
[13] "Brain Oscillations"   "Brain Hemodynamics"   "Addiction/Cravings"
```

:::
:::


Run this cell below to check for each unique combination of study_id + condition + outcome_cat appearing exactly once. If it returns 0, it is clean and deduped. 

::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
df_clean |>
  count(study_id, condition, outcome_cat) |>
  filter(n > 1)
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
# A tibble: 0 × 4
# ℹ 4 variables: study_id <chr>, condition <chr>, outcome_cat <chr>, n <int>
```


:::
